zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Blake of Quemberford M.P.於1412年出生在英國維爾爾卡爾內時，他的父親Henry Blake28歲，母親Margaret Bellett27歲。他於1429年與Avice Wallop在英國英格蘭維爾爾卡爾內結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1474年10月29日在英國英格蘭維爾爾享年62歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭維爾爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Blake of Quemberford M.P.于1412年出生在英国維爾爾卡爾內时，他的父亲Henry Blake28岁，母亲Margaret Bellett27岁。他于1429年与Avice Wallop在英国英格兰維爾爾卡爾內结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1474年10月29日在英国英格兰維爾爾享年62岁，埋葬在英国英格兰維爾爾。

nameMeanings.name: Blake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (England and central Scotland): variant of Black 1, meaning ‘swarthy’ or ‘dark-haired’, from a byform of the Old English adjective blæc, blac ‘black’, with change of vowel length.

English: nickname from Middle English blak(e) (Old English blāc) ‘wan, pale, white, fair’. In Middle English the two words blac and blāc, with opposite meanings, fell together as Middle English blake. In the absence of independent evidence as to whether the person referred to was dark or fair, it is now impossible to tell which sense was originally meant.
English (Norfolk): nickname from Middle English bleik, blaik>, blek(e) (Old Norse bleikr) ‘pale or sallow’ (in complexion).

variants.name: Bleak
meanings: English: mostly a variant of   Blake  , but perhaps also of   Blick  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Black
meanings: English and Scottish: chiefly from Middle English blak(e) ‘black’ (Old English blæc, blaca), a nickname given from the earliest times to a swarthy or dark-haired man. However, Middle English blac also meant ‘pale, wan’, a reflex of Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ with a shortened vowel. Compare Blatch and Blick . With rare exceptions it is impossible to disambiguate these antithetical senses in Middle English surnames. The same difficulty arises with Blake and Block .
Scottish: in Gaelic-speaking areas this name was adopted as a translation of the epithet dubh ‘dark, black-(haired)’, or of various other names based on Gaelic dubh ‘black’, see Duff .

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames directly or indirectly derived from the adjective meaning ‘black, dark’, for example German and Jewish Schwarz and Slavic surnames beginning with Čern-, Chern- (see Chern and Cherne ), Chorn-, Crn- or Czern-.

variants.name: Blaikie
meanings: Scottish: nickname from a pet form of   Blake  .

variants.name: Blakes
meanings: English: variant of Blake , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: M.P.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Chung, Chang, Jian, Li, Tong, Bin, Chao, Cheng, Chin, Chuan, Der, Feng, Fung, Hua.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 穆, meaning ‘solemnity, virtue’: (i) from Mu (穆), posthumous title of the Duke Mu of Song (died 720  BC ), the 14th ruler of the state of Song (located in present-day Shangqiu in Henan province). He earned the title Mu, 穆, because of his virtue during his reign. (ii) from the Qiu Mu Ling (丘穆陵) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient northern China, who changed their original surname to Mu (穆) during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from Mu (穆), posthumous title of the Duke Mu of Lu (died 377  BC ), ruler of the state of Lu (located in present-day Shandong province).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 慕: (i) said to be traced back to Yu Mu (虞慕), ancestor of the legendary Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Mu-Rong (慕容), a surname from the Xianbei and Tuyuhun ethnic group in northern China. (iii) a surname from Korean and Jurchen ethnic groups in northeastern China.

variants.name: Moo
meanings: Chinese: variant of   Mu  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 巫, possibly based on its Hakka pronunciation, see   Wu   4.
Burmese (Karen): variant of   Mu   9.

variants.name: Wu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ming, Wei, Jian, Mei, Jing, Ying, Bin, Hong, Jin, Ping, Ching, Wen.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 吳, meaning ‘big, large’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Wu (吳), the name of a state (located in present-day Jiangsu province) which was granted to Tai Bo, the first son of King Tai of Zhou (died c. 1146 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Yue in 473 BC , people from the state adopted 吳 (Wu) as their surname. (ii) from the placename Yu (虞) (located in present-day Shanxi province). Because Yu (虞) had a similar pronunciation to that of Wu (吳) in ancient Chinese, some people acquired Wu (吳) instead of Yu (虞) as their surname.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Blake of Quemberford M.P. was born in 1412, in Calne, Wiltshire, England, his father, Henry Blake, was 28 and his mother, Margaret Bellett, was 27. He married Avice Wallop in 1429, in Calne, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 29 October 1474, in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 62, and was buried in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Blake of Quemberford M.P. est né en 1412, à Calne, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry Blake, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Margaret Bellett, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Avice Wallop en 1429, à Calne, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 29 octobre 1474, à Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Blake of Quemberford M.P. 1412 in Calne, Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry Blake 28 und seine Mutter Margaret Bellett 27. Er hat 1429 in Calne, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom Avice Wallop geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 29. Oktober 1474 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Blake of Quemberford M.P. è nato il nel 1412 nel Calne, Wiltshire, England, il suo padre, Henry Blake, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Bellett, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Avice Wallop nel 1429, nel Calne, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 29 ottobre 1474, nel Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Blake of Quemberford M.P.が1412年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアカルンで生まれた時，父Henry Blakeは28歳，母Margaret Bellettは27歳であった。1429年にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアカルンでAvice Wallopと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1474年10月29日にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアで62歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Blake of Quemberford M.P.(이)가 1412년 영국 윌츠히르 캘니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Blake(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Bellett(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1429년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 캘니에서 Avice Wallop(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1474년 10월 29일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Blake of Quemberford M.P. nasceu em 1412, em Calne, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry Blake, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Bellett, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Avice Wallop em 1429, em Calne, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 29 de outubro de 1474, em Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Blake of Quemberford M.P. родился в 1412 г. в местности калн, уилцхир, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry Blake было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Bellett, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Avice Wallop в 1429 г. в местности калн, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 29 октября 1474 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Blake of Quemberford M.P. nació en 1412, en Calne, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry Blake, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Margaret Bellett, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Avice Wallop en 1429, en Calne, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 29 de octubre de 1474, en Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
